latest_post_merge_uat.timing_note: ResourceEnvelope full 43-query local Desktop UAT after shared memory/parallelism/capillary/state/top-K/writer policy evidence was wired through native Vortex routes; not official ClickBench submission
latest_post_merge_uat.next_optimization_focus: remaining >10s local UAT rows are high-cardinality/string top-K, transformed URL/domain grouping, bounded wide-row top-K, string predicate count, and exact distinct; preserve shared Vortex-normalized physical runtime, ResourceEnvelope policy, single .vortex artifact, no sidecars, no fallback, and no external engine execution

command_shape: target/release/shardloom run sql --input <hits.vortex> --input-format vortex --sql <clickbench-query> --request collect --bounded true --memory-gb 8 --max-parallelism 2 --format json

current_replacement_artifact_uat.artifact_shape_tradeoff: 2026-06-27 replacement UAT produced one 34.9GB .vortex artifact with 36,660 footer segments and no query-answer sidecars. Prepare wall time was 515.47s; engine evidence attributes 477,811ms to prepare, 455,636ms to Vortex write, and 454,059ms to segment write. Treat this as local UAT evidence, not a load-speed closeout or official ClickBench submission.

optimization_families.strategy: row-reference/selection-vector top-K with prepared predicate and payload locality; final materialization only for retained rows
dictionary-aware exact distinct with dense IDs/bitsets where exact and encoded-key merge before decode
partitioned capillary partial state, packed typed/composite keys, memory-budgeted exact merge, and spill diagnostics for near-unique groups
embedded generic string/domain metadata, segment absence certificates, byte-length/domain dictionaries, and exact predicate masks before materialization

families.dominant_cost_class: exact distinct state rebuilt at query time
families.query_time_goal: merge exact prepared distinct state before decoded value materialization
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL COUNT(DISTINCT), Python/DataFrame nunique/value_counts/distinct, and CLI SQL all select the same native_vortex_unified_plan distinct contract

families.dominant_cost_class: large or near-unique group state rebuilt at query time
families.query_time_goal: consume exact prepared partial state and merge retained candidates instead of scanning all rows into one hot map
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL GROUP BY, Python/DataFrame group_by().agg(), nlargest/nsmallest, and CLI SQL all share the prepared aggregate/top-K route

families.dominant_cost_class: runtime string scan, host extraction, or string-key retention
families.query_time_goal: prune from prepared exact string metadata and group on encoded IDs before decoding retained output strings
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL LIKE/REGEXP_REPLACE/length and Python/DataFrame contains/replace/length/domain helpers all use the same prepared string route

families.dominant_cost_class: wide row payload reread or materialization before final retained row set
families.query_time_goal: retain row references through sort/top-K and materialize only final output rows
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL ORDER BY LIMIT/OFFSET, Python/DataFrame sort/head/tail/nlargest, and CLI SQL all share the same late-materialized top-N route

families.dominant_cost_class: derived expression recomputation across aggregate/update paths
families.query_time_goal: evaluate reusable exact derived columns once per prepared state or once per retained capillary unit
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL expressions and Python/DataFrame expression builders use the same expression fingerprint and derived-column admission path

prepared_olap_reuse_plan.query_time_consumption_status: implemented_whole_file_footer_pruning_for_count_filter_aggregate_and_bounded_sort_local_primitives_via_VortexFile_can_prune; deeper segment/string/domain metadata-first pruning, dictionary/layout policy, and broader ClickBench-heavy planner consumption remain planned runtime optimization work
prepared_olap_reuse_plan.shared_runtime_contract: Universal Ingest -> SourceState -> VortexPreparedState -> prepared_olap_state when available -> native_vortex_unified_plan -> typed result or sink
prepared_olap_reuse_plan.single_artifact_storage_model: one prepared .vortex artifact; no adjacent prepared-OLAP manifest, .prepared-olap-state.d directory, or query-summary sidecar is allowed on the public/default runtime path

rejected_prepared_olap_query_summary_sid.reason: Exact query-summary sidecars behave like query-contract warm state and are not the ShardLoom product path. Public/default runtime uses one prepared .vortex artifact with embedded/generic Vortex layout and statistics evidence; external prepared-OLAP manifests and sidecar directories are disabled.

targeted_current_guardrails.ingest_ship_drop_target: Current accepted local 100M Parquet replacement UAT verifies source-to-Vortex normalization, source-text dictionary-Zstd writer coverage, single .vortex artifact output, embedded OLAP/layout evidence, no external manifest/sidecar files, no hidden temp files after completion, and fallback/external-engine=false. Next ingest work is writer-profile tuning from this evidence: reduce vortex_segment_write_millis without losing the single-artifact/no-sidecar contract or increasing final artifact size materially.

rows.reason: Pure URL contains count remains dominated by full string scan work; this confirms no regression but does not close the string predicate family.
Mixed pushdown plus residual contains route remains correct and slightly faster, but still needs embedded string/domain metadata or segment pruning to move the dominant cost class.

latest_replacement_ingest_guard.next_action: Do not make a load-time claim from this attempt. Either run a safety-only CPU-throttled local check with no performance claim, adjust the local guard policy to allow one fully used core, or further redesign the writer/source pipeline before replacement UAT.

targeted_current_guardrails.open_conclusion: Current-branch targeted substitutions now project the full-43 total at 290.766s and geomean at 2.303911s before the next full UAT rerun. Retained highlights include Q33 numeric-pair late-measure at 4.051s, Q15/Q17 numeric+UTF8 chunk-compacted heavy-hitter routes at 9.698s/17.525s, Q18 source-order numeric+UTF8 candidate-slot counting at 4.575s, Q21 count-only chunk-dictionary contains at 9.414s, Q22/Q23/Q24 current string-predicate routes at 7.388s/13.399s/11.475s, Q28 encoded numeric dictionary access at 15.65s, Q29 transformed dictionary measures at 16.446s, and Q34/Q35 string heavy-hitter routes at 29.804s/30.07s. Remaining double-digit families are URL string top-K, numeric+UTF8 heavy-hitter, transformed-string grouping, exact distinct, bounded sort/materialization, and ingest/load throughput. These still require generic layout/dictionary/posting-list/state work rather than query-specific answer caches.

rejected_changes_after_targeted_uat.reason: Storing transformed-dictionary general aggregate group values as reconstructable from the group key removed duplicate group-value materialization evidence, but targeted 100M UAT was slower than the retained Q29 transformed-dictionary measure route. The runtime code path was removed before shipment; future Q29-class work must reduce transform scan volume, dictionary/posting metadata cost, or aggregate measure update work rather than only removing cold result-state duplication.

Building exact recount candidate-code maps from existing interner IDs avoided reinterning non-candidates, but targeted 100M UAT did not improve the retained proof-bound URL top-K route. The code path was removed before shipment; future URL top-K work must reduce a larger cost class such as source string scans, dictionary/posting metadata, or exact recount pass volume.

Maintaining the retained candidate window on every near-unique numeric-pair group update added hot-loop work and caused the 100M Q33 route to exceed the 180-second UAT cap; the runtime code path was removed before shipment.
Counting the FSST LIKE bool mask directly did not reduce the current artifact path and regressed the URL LIKE count row; deeper prepared string/domain metadata remains the required optimization.

Replacing the shared dictionary contains count/index helpers with a lighter conversion/count loop did not improve the current 100M artifact. Q21 regressed against the latest full-43 lane timing and Q24 was slightly slower while still scanning the same URL predicate candidates. The code path was removed before shipment; future string-predicate work should reduce scan volume or consume richer embedded dictionary/posting metadata instead of only shaving per-row flag lookup overhead.

Splitting the near-unique numeric-pair group map into fixed partitions added routing/merge overhead without reducing the dominant 99,997,493-group state volume; the code path was removed before shipment.

The candidate did not activate a faster route on the current Vortex artifact and the existing URLHash column is not an exact functional surrogate for URL grouping: a bounded sample found multiple URLHash values for the same URL. The runtime code and tests were removed before shipment. Future URL grouping work must use exact embedded domain/dictionary metadata or source-derived columns created during Universal Ingest with source-hash invalidation.

retained_changes.shared_component: native Vortex string top-K and count-distinct top-K exact recount
retained_changes.decision_gate: retained because targeted CB-Q34 improved and CB-Q35 was not measurably slower on the current 100M artifact; drop if later full-43 UAT shows route regression
retained_changes.reason: The current public workflow still lifts string candidate signature prefilter evidence, but the local primitive had lost the producer after the interner-ID refactor. The restored path builds compact length/prefix/suffix signatures from exact heavy-hitter candidate IDs, screens dictionary values before candidate ID lookup, and still performs exact interned-ID verification before counts or distinct sets are updated. It does not add routes, sidecars, query-answer caches, or external fallback.

focused_validation: cargo clippy -q -p shardloom-cli --features vortex-write --test generated_source_runtime_smoke -- -D warnings
cargo clippy -q -p shardloom-cli --features release-user-surfaces --bin shardloom -- -D warnings
retained_changes.decision_gate: retained because it reuses existing VortexPreparedStateWriteReport fields and exposes the 455s segment-write cost class on generated-source .vortex output without adding runtime work, routes, sidecars, or external execution
retained_changes.reason: The replacement-ingest decision gate needs shared writer-pressure evidence before more writer-profile changes. SQL-local source paths already expose the split; generated-source Vortex output now carries the same segment-write, workspace-stage, writer profile, and layout-decision vocabulary from the common report surface.

retained_changes.decision_gate: retained because it records why the shared writer selected small-source, non-text fast-load, large text fast-Zstd, or very-large text coalesced profiles and records the guard against previously rejected uncompressed text artifacts and dictionary-training CPU without changing output semantics or adding hot-loop cost
retained_changes.reason: The 100M replacement-ingest long pole is writer/segment work. The previous UAT sequence proved some writer profiles are materially slower or produce unacceptable artifact size. This evidence lets the next replacement ingest compare like-for-like profile decisions across public surfaces before changing writer behavior.

retained_changes.shared_component: vortex-run local primitive execution report renderer
retained_changes.decision_gate: retained because it proves the existing generic local_primitive_* evidence surface carries optimized physical-route state across SQL/Python/DataFrame/CLI wrappers without adding surface-specific fields or runtime work
retained_changes.reason: The maintainer clarified that surface choice should be preference-level syntax. The shared renderer now has focused coverage for row-ref top-K, encoded exact distinct, and grouped aggregate partial/merge evidence so ClickBench UAT optimizations remain attached to the Vortex-normalized physical route rather than to one public front door.

affected_families_expected: SQL over .vortex ORDER BY LIMIT top-K
SQL over .vortex primitive row export sinks
retained_changes.shared_component: Python SQL/DataFrame lowering -> public workflow native_vortex primitive payloads
retained_changes.decision_gate: retained because SQL DISTINCT, simple SQL ORDER BY ... LIMIT, and SQL primitive JSONL/CSV row-export payloads now lower into the same native Vortex primitive families as equivalent DataFrame operations instead of requiring a separate Vortex SQL route or external query-engine integration
retained_changes.reason: This closes a route-unification gap identified during the Vortex SQL architecture audit. It is not a query timing claim; it keeps surface choice at the syntax layer so future distinct/top-K/sink optimizations land once in the shared Vortex-normalized primitive runtime.

Medium/large URL/text columnar Universal Ingest sources now choose the full embedded derived-column wrapper instead of the source-native-only wrapper, so prepared .vortex artifacts can carry exact hidden length and URL-domain columns even when the Parquet/Arrow/ORC reader surfaces plain UTF-8. The full adapter now emits compact dictionary/code length metadata plus dictionary URL-domain metadata in one source-string pass rather than raw per-row hidden length values. Native Vortex aggregate rewrites consume those hidden columns for url_domain/length groups and measures, and dictionary-backed hidden domain groups with direct numeric hidden length measures now aggregate by dictionary-code partials before final group materialization. This is not counted as a timing win until replacement ingest and targeted URL-domain UAT prove that the added write cost reduces query cost.

Numeric+UTF8 proof-bound heavy-hitter routes now compact repeated chunk-local dictionary-code keys into weighted sketch updates and chunk-local exact recount merges. The path stays on the shared native Vortex aggregate route, uses the same single .vortex artifact, and reports no fallback or external engine execution.

The route now builds exact count state first, retains the bounded top-K numeric-pair keys, and reopens the same single .vortex artifact to materialize SUM/AVG measures only for retained keys. Targeted 100M UAT showed exact result-row parity with the prior one-pass route while reducing Q33 from the 127-173s range to 14.86s.

The route uses a generic exact proof-bound string top-K operator: first-pass weighted heavy-hitter candidate sketch over chunk UTF-8 dictionaries, lower-bound proof before exact recount, second-pass candidate-only recount on the same single .vortex artifact, and native exact fallback if proof is not possible. It preserved exact result-row parity for CB-Q34/CB-Q35 while reducing the current URL grouping path by roughly 63-66%.

The current branch retains the generic proof-bound URL count top-K path on the same single .vortex artifact, with chunk UTF-8 dictionary accessors, candidate-state recount, no fallback, and no external engine execution.

The SQL shape SELECT 1, URL, COUNT(*) GROUP BY 1, URL now treats the literal as a reconstructable dependent group expression, so the real URL key can reuse the generic proof-bound string count top-K path instead of building full exact group state for every URL. The output still emits the literal column and preserves exact native Vortex/no-fallback evidence.

Encoded numeric dictionary codes and values now use the Vortex primitive execution boundary before ShardLoom aggregate accessors are chosen, so numeric dictionary group keys such as CounterID no longer materialize to StatValue rows while URL length measures remain dictionary-backed. This is a shared native Vortex accessor improvement for dictionary-coded numeric group keys, not a ClickBench-only route.

The unordered GROUP BY UserID, SearchPhrase LIMIT K shape still keeps the existing source-order admission contract, but once the retained source-order group window closes it counts only retained numeric+UTF8 dictionary candidates by code and merges chunk counts into the existing retained groups. This avoids per-row generic AggregateGroupKey probes without introducing query-answer sidecars or fallback execution.

The current branch retains native_vortex_count_where over the count-only chunk-dictionary contains path. The probe scanned the single .vortex artifact with streaming local primitive execution, selected 15911 rows, and reported no decode/materialization of result rows.
The Vortex primitive decode probe still materialized most hot columns and was slower than the current numeric-pair path; the code path was removed before shipment.

Exact comparison, null, and IN-list residual predicates now select counts/row indices from projected Vortex columns through typed, dictionary, or materialized accessors and can feed exact candidate row indices directly into scalar/grouped aggregate accessors without selected-row export or predicate re-check. Offset-heavy count-star grouped top-K now switches from a linear retained-window scan to capillary select-nth retention when offset+limit exceeds 128, preserving deterministic final sort while avoiding O(groups*offset) candidate comparisons. Mixed residual predicates that are not fully exact still materialize and re-check candidates.

comparison_to_prior_source_text_success.conclusion: artifact shape improved materially, but prepare time regressed; keep load-speed tuning open.

previous_rejected_replacement_attempt.rejection_reason: bounded Capillary Arrow-to-Vortex normalization prefetch plus narrow text compression improved materially versus all-column balanced compression, but it still did not complete a 100M Parquet replacement within the three-minute local UAT cap. Temp output reached 2.54GB and was cleaned up; no artifact was retained.
previous_rejected_replacement_attempt.fix_after_rejection: Keep the bounded Capillary normalization-worker pipeline and replace the narrow writer shape with source-schema text dictionary-Zstd field policy before retaining a replacement artifact.

prior_rejected_replacement_attempts.rejection_reason: all-column balanced BtrBlocks/Zstd-compatible large-source writer profile made only 145845496 temp bytes after 191.341 seconds

bounded Capillary Arrow-to-Vortex normalization prefetch made 100M Parquet replacement ingest materially faster, but the uncompressed fast-load large-source writer profile ballooned the artifact to about 50GiB
ordered per-RecordBatch Parquet row-group emission alone did not materially improve 100M Parquet replacement ingest
retained custom 65,536-row / 1MiB large-source writer strategy was still too slow before source/writer overlap redesign
upstream-default writer substitution produced only about 46MiB temp output before stop and was slower than the retained custom writer path
default physical derived-column synthesis still made 100M Parquet replacement ingest progress too slowly after the writer/coalescer experiment was removed
physical Arrow batch coalescing plus a 262,144-row/8 MiB writer target was slower than the retained replacement-ingest pace

previous_rejected_replacement_attempt.next_action: Keep source-schema text dictionary-Zstd as the current single-artifact correctness baseline and continue writer-profile timing work before making any load-speed claim.

latest_resource_envelope_decision.targeted_delta_interpretation: Targeted repeat shows the slower full43 ResourceEnvelope total was concentrated in run variance/cold-cache effects: Q34/Q35/Q29/Q24/Q19 improved versus post-merge #1361, Q17/Q23 were flat, and Q21/Q33 remain the only material slower candidates in this repeated set.

latest_resource_envelope_targeted_delta.purpose: repeat the full43 ResourceEnvelope run's largest apparent regressions to separate route slowdown from run/cache variance before PR
latest_resource_envelope_targeted_delta.readout: Targeted repeat shows the slower full43 ResourceEnvelope total was concentrated in run variance/cold-cache effects: Q34/Q35/Q29/Q24/Q19 improved versus post-merge #1361, Q17/Q23 were flat, and Q21/Q33 remain the only material slower candidates in this repeated set.
latest_resource_envelope_targeted_delta.remaining_pre_merge_optimization_decisio: No revert. Ship ResourceEnvelope evidence plumbing and lazy top-K path, then continue optimizing Q21 string predicate pruning and Q33 high-cardinality numeric-pair retained-measure state in follow-up performance work.

latest_runtime_driver_targeted_uat.purpose: validate the shared Vortex current-thread worker-pool runtime driver on changed count/filter and bounded sort/top-K route families without running full 43-query UAT
comparison_notes: CB-Q21 improved versus latest ResourceEnvelope full43 evidence (12.732s) and targeted repeat (10.627s), and was effectively flat versus post-merge #1361 full43 (9.290s).
CB-Q24 improved versus latest ResourceEnvelope full43 evidence (21.349s), targeted repeat (14.443s), and post-merge #1361 full43 (15.136s).
CB-Q25/CB-Q26/CB-Q27 improved versus latest ResourceEnvelope full43 evidence and post-merge #1361 full43.
CB-Q01 remained metadata-preserving with zero arrays read; its residual wall time is classified as front-door process/startup/file-open overhead rather than operator runtime.
latest_runtime_driver_targeted_uat.decision: ship_for_scan_export_sort_route_families; keep metadata-only count and aggregate state routes on prior runtime boundary until separately proven
latest_runtime_driver_targeted_uat.follow_up: evaluate persistent local worker/session and footer/open cache for tiny metadata queries and Python/CLI wrapper loops; do not confuse that overhead with row-scan/operator runtime

ingest_parallelism_evidence.follow_up: source-side Parquet capillary read parallelism is not fully consuming the two-worker envelope; optimize without changing the single-artifact or no-fallback contract

comparison_to_latest_resource_envelope_f.readout: overall query wall improved while geomean regressed slightly; treat this as local UAT evidence and slow-lane prioritization, not a public performance claim

latest_full_ingest_and_query_uat.decision: ship_functional_route_readiness_and_runtime_driver_batch; continue optimizing writer-dominant ingest, source-side capillary parallelism, string dictionary recount lanes, and row-ref top-k variance before release claims
latest_full_ingest_and_query_uat.release_surface_note: successful UAT required building shardloom-cli with --features release-user-surfaces; vortex-traditional-analytics-benchmark alone blocks public Parquet preparation by design
